game_name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic movement and observation commands in starting location
Collecting items needed for puzzles; managing limited inventory
Combining items, using objects in correct sequence; trial-and-error learning
Managing action economy; understanding that most commands consume turns
Asking guide about topics to get hints; expanding puzzle-solving toolkit
Completing objectives within turn limits or facing game over; requires restart from checkpoint
Manipulating events across multiple locations and sequences; retroactive causality puzzle (affecting past to change present)
New location with multiple interior areas; navigation and puzzle-solving in confined spaces
Understanding improbability mechanics; requires creative problem-solving to power and utilize
Access to restricted areas; final puzzle chain prerequisite
Final major obstacle requiring synthesis of earlier puzzle solutions

typical_relief_triggers: Progressing to a new location or scene
Getting a hint from the Hitchhiker's Guide
Safe passage or non-hostile NPC interaction
typical_tension_triggers: Turn counter approaching limits (game over threats)
Needing to manipulate past events (non-obvious causality)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradually escalating puzzle difficulty with turn-limit pressure; early game emphasizes exploration and item collection, mid-game introduces interconnected puzzles and time pressure, late-game demands synthesis of all previous mechanics; comedic tone provides emotional relief from mechanical difficulty

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Examining objects with 'look' command reveals hidden details and puzzle solutions; Guide lookups provide optional hints

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to text-based commands and basic inventory mechanics; primary challenge is learning the interface and understanding that observations are not turn-consuming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate escalation; introduction of multi-step puzzles, turn-limited objectives, and the need to consult the Hitchhiker's Guide for hints; player must synthesize information across locations
difficulty_curve.late_game: Steep difficulty spike; final puzzle requires manipulating past events of other characters to bring items into the present, then using those items to power the ship's systems; ship landing is blocked until computer is fixed, requiring access to restricted area (Marvin's closet)
notable_spikes: First room: 'deviousness' reputation suggests early tutorial may be unexpectedly difficult
Tea substitute puzzle: requires understanding of Brownian motion and improbability mechanics
Marvin's closet access: gaining permission to reach restricted tools
Final computer fix sequence: synthesizes all previous puzzle knowledge and mechanic understanding

notable_level_patterns.description: Opening narrative setup (safe context); introduces core premise and escape mechanic; establishes turn-counting stakes
Survival scenario (moderate danger); poetry reading survival, airlock escape by improbability; demonstrates that success can come from luck/absurdity rather than pure logic
Confined spatial puzzle-solving; multiple interconnected rooms; sauna departure pattern (NPCs leave, player must solve problem alone); introduces inventory management complexity
Complex causal puzzle; player manipulates past events of three characters (Ford, Zaphod, Trillian) to retroactively place items aboard ship in present; requires understanding non-linear causality
Time-limited response puzzle; missiles converted to whale/petunias using Improbability Drive; immediate tactical problem requiring resource application
Gated progression; computer blocks landing, NPCs unavailable, player must reach Marvin's closet for final tools; synthesizes turn management, inventory, navigation, and NPC interaction mechanics
